We first need to construct the probability distribution for X . We use the card and coin events to determine the probability for each outcome, but we use the monetary value of X to determine the expected value. \begin{table}[h]

\hline
Card Event & Xnet gain/loss & P(X) \\ \hline
Face Card and Heads & 6 & $$( \frac{12}{52} ) ( \frac{1}{2} ) = ( \frac{6}{52} )$$ \\ \hline
Face Card and Tails & 2 & $$( \frac{12}{52} ) ( \frac{1}{2} ) = ( \frac{6}{52} )$$ \\ \hline
(Not Face Card) and (H or T) & -2 & $$( \frac{40}{52} ) ( 1 ) = ( \frac{40}{52} )$$ \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table} $$\text{Expected value} = ( 6 ) ( \frac{6}{52} ) + ( 2 ) ( \frac{6}{52} ) + ( - 2 ) ( \frac{40}{52} ) = - \frac{32}{52}$$ Expected value = -$0.62, rounded to the nearest cent If you play this game repeatedly, over a long string of games, you would expect to lose 62 cents per game, on average.
